_Order Bumps allow you to offer additional products to your students during their checkout experience, without slowing down the overall checkout process!_

_This feature, and other TCommerce selling tools, are **only available when using Thinkific Payments** as your primary payment processor._

_Get started with Thinkific Payments by clicking below:_

[Set Up Thinkific Payments](https://courses.thinkific.com/onboarding/signin?redirect_to=/manage/settings#tab-payments)

## About Order Bumps

Order Bumps are a way to offer additional products in the checkout when your students purchase one product. Order Bumps take advantage of the existing motivation and momentum of the primary sale to offer a related product: it could be digital resources or tools, additional one-on-one coaching sessions, a community, or even a course. They do this while keeping the checkout experience simple and straightforward, students simply check a box to add the product, and both purchases are handled in a single transaction.

Order Bumps are available exclusively to creators on [TCommerce: Powered by Thinkific Payments](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/7302902582807). To access Order Bumps as well as other great TCommerce features, enable Thinkific Payments on your account: [Thinkific Payments](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360062084913).

Setup Order Bumps

Click for sound

1:17

## How It Works

There are two products involved in an order bump:

- _The primary product_ refers to the original product that the student has selected to buy - the main product they have added to the checkout to purchase.
- The _order bump_ is the complementary product that is offered during checkout, when the primary product is being purchased.

Any existing product on Thinkific can be offered as an Order Bump, as long as it is:

- [Published](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030738133) and
- Not [set to Private](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030738053)

Similarly, Order Bumps can be added to any existing primary products, as long as these are published and not set to private.

### The Checkout Experience

Once you have added an order bump to a primary product, students who purchase that primary product will see it offered at the checkout. They will be able to select the order bump product, and have this added to their purchase with a single transaction.

In the example below, you can see that the order bump product is offered at the checkout for the primary product (the Mastering Pizza course).

![TCommerce Checkout - Apr 24.jpg](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/26760560095895)

### After the Purchase

Once a student has purchased an order bump, they will automatically and immediately gain access to both the primary product and the order bump.

Access will be provided to each product individually, the same way it would if the student had purchased the products separately. This automatic enrolment will also trigger any welcome emails or other processes you have set up for both the primary product and the order bump.

To see how an order bump will appear or work, you can temporarily set the primary product (the product you are adding the order bump to) to [hidden](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030738053#hidden), to test the new order bump without anyone accidentally viewing or purchasing it.

For a more complete description, see [The Student Experience](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/4410781476631-Sell-More-with-Order-Bumps#studentexperience) section below.

## Important Considerations

- You can only have one order bump added to any given primary product! If a primary product already has an order bump added to it, this primary product will not appear in the list of available products when creating a new order bump.
- Order Bumps have their own price, allowing you to offer a discount to students who are already buying one of your other products.
- [Sales Tax and VAT](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551) are calculated on Order Bump products.
- Products priced with recurring payments ( [subscription](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360034692814) or [payment plan](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030722113)) cannot be bought using PayPal as a payment method, as per existing Paypal limitations.
- Order Bumps cannot be combined with [Gifting](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/17911685455127) or [Group Order](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/20508209851671) features. If an order bump is added to the product, the gifting and group order options will not be available.
- When you create an order bump then delete the product that was added to it, the order bump will be automatically deleted as well.

## Order Bump Fields

How you craft your order bump to grab the attention of your students is an important consideration when growing your business. Check out some of our [tips on crafting an effective Order Bump](https://www.thinkific.com/order-bump-tips-tricks/)!

Order bumps include the follow information:

- **Title:** this will appear at the top of the order bump card
- **Product card image:** this will be auto-populated from your product
- **Product description:** a longer description of the product that will appear below the _Title_
- **Price:** the price of the order bump — what the student will be charged if they decide to add it to their purchase
- **Strikethrough price:** if set, the _Strikethrough price_ will appear next to the price, with a line through it, as a way of suggesting that the order bump is being offered at a discount

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/5430186739351)

\\* This uses the [product card image for the course, community or bundle](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360035064553)

## Create an Order Bump

> At this time, Order Bump products priced as a recurring payment ( [subscription](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360034692814) or [payment plan](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030722113)) cannot be added to a primary product priced as a recurring payment. We’re always working behind the scenes on feature improvements and if you have feedback on this, we want to know. Please send us a quick email to let us know what functionalities are most valuable to you!

1. From your Admin Dashboard, select **Sales**
2. Select **Order bumps**
3. Click the **Create order bump** button
4. Select the product you would like to offer as the order bump under _Search for and select your product_
5. Click the **Add order bumps to the following products** button and select one or more products that you want to add this order bump to. Select from the list of the 25 most recently published products or use a search bar to find the right product.
6. Click **Confirm** to confirm the products that the order bump will be added to
7. Add a _Price_ and, optionally, a _Strikethrough price_
8. Add _Order bump details_, which will be displayed in the checkout where the order bump appears
9. Click the **Save** button on the top right

Having trouble seeing your order bump on your checkout page after creation? Check out our [troubleshooting guide](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/4410782261911)!

## Edit an Existing Order Bump

1. From your Admin Dashboard, select **Sales**
2. Select **Order bumps**
3. Find the order bump you want to edit in the list of existing order bumps and click the **Edit** button for that order bump
4. Make the changes needed
5. Click the **Save** button on the top right

## Delete an Existing Order Bump

1. From your Admin Dashboard, select **Sales**
2. Select **Order bumps**
3. Find the order bump you want to delete in the list of existing order bumps and click the **ellipsis** button to the right
4. Click **Delete**
5. Confirm your decision by clicking **Delete**

Once confirmed, the deleted order bump will no longer appear on your list of order bumps, nor will it appear in the checkout.

If you prefer not to delete the order bump but do not want it to be active on any of your checkouts, you can find the order bump you want to remove in the list of existing order bumps and click the **Edit** button for that order bump. Then, open the _Add order bumps to the following products_ dropdown menu and remove all selected products. You can also do this if you want to temporarily disable an order bump that you might want to use again at a later date.

## The Student Experience

Order bumps are designed to be as intuitive as possible for the student, following patterns established by cart-based checkout processes across the internet. When the student decides to purchase a product that has an order bump offer attached to it, they will see a section at the bottom of the checkout page with the order bump card. To accept the offer, the student can select the checkbox and have the order bump product added to their order summary.

Once the student completes their order, the following events will occur:

- The student is charged the full order amount, which appears as a single charge on their bank account statement
- The student is automatically enrolled in both products
- The student will receive a single receipt (if you have enabled [Student Order Receipts](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360035210354)) which will show an itemized list of both the primary product and the order bump
- The student will receive a [Welcome Email](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030352074) for each product they have enrolled in, that has a welcome email enabled
- The order will appear in the student's order history and details, which they can view from their [Student Dashboard](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500001538961)

## View and Track Order Bump Purchases

Once a purchase, including an order bump, is complete, it appears in the _Orders_ table much like any other purchase. The purchase will appear as a single order, with a total amount equal to the total of the primary product and the order bump.

To make it easier to determine which orders included an order bump — and which order bump it was — the Orders table includes an _Order Bump_ column. This column lists the product name of the order bump.

In addition, the Order details will display the primary product and order bump (if any), with a line item showing the individual amounts for each.

Additionally, you can use the _TCommerce Feature = Order Bump_ filter in the [Revenue Dashboard](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093#revenue) to analyze the transactions that used Order Bumps.

## Refund Order Bumps

Transactions that include an order bump can be refunded normally: take a look at our article on [Thinkific Payments: Refunds](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360056382214) for more details. You can capture a partial refund if you need to return only the order bump amount. Remember that refunds do not automatically unenroll a student: you will have to [do so manually](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351314-How-to-Unenroll-Students-and-Process-Refunds#unenrollfromcourse) if you wish them to be unenrolled.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Order Bumps Set Up

**I really want to use Order Bumps, but TCommerce isn't available in my country — is there some way I can get access without using TCommerce?**

Order Bumps are currently only available for creators using our [TCommerce](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/7302902582807) suite of tools, which includes [Thinkific Payments](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360062084913).

**Who has access to the Order Bumps menu? What user roles can create Order Bumps?**

Only [Site Owners, Site Admins, and External Partners](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030353794) are able to view, create, edit, or remove order bumps.

**Can I offer a product exclusively as an order bump?**

Yes! If you want to make something available exclusively as an order bump, you can use our [Hidden feature](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030738053) [.](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030738053) Setting the product as hidden will ensure that no one comes across it accidentally while browsing your site, but will still make it available to use as an order bump.

**Can I add an order bump to a free product/bundle?**

Yes! Free products or bundles can have an order bump added to them.

**Can I add a pre-order product as an order bump?**

No, pre-order products are not currently available for order bumps. You also cannot add an order bump to a pre-order product. Your product must be [published](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030738133).

**What happens if I delete a product that is part of an order bump?**

When you create an order bump then delete the product that was added to it, the order bump will be automatically deleted as well.

### Order Bumps and Other Features

**How do order bumps work with Affiliates? Will they get credit for an order bump?**

While this can get tricky depending on how you have set up your [affiliate](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030370514) commissions, the short answer is that commissions are calculated only based on the primary product purchased, regardless of whether or not the student decides to add an order bump to the transaction.

_For example:_ an Affiliate receives a 10% commission on all sales of a product, which sells for $100. A student decides to buy the product and then, during checkout, also includes a $25 order bump in their purchase. The Affiliate would receive $10 (10% of $100) — the order bump would not be included in the calculation.

This is also true for flat per-purchase commissions: an order bump does not count as an additional purchase when calculating Affiliate payouts.

**How will order bumps work with Revenue Partners? What happens if I offer the course at a discount as an order bump?**

Since [Revenue Partner](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030370454) commissions are always a percentage of a given product, the interaction is relatively straightforward: a Revenue Partner will receive a share of revenue only for the products they have been assigned, regardless of whether they are offered as the primary product _or_ as an order bump.

_For example:_ a Revenue Partner receives 20% commission on the sales for Product A, which is offered at a regular/primary price of $100. A student purchases Product A at the regular price, then adds a $50 order bump (for Product B), for a total transaction of $150. The Partner would receive $20, because that is their share of the payment for Product A; the order bump is for a different product, so it is not included.

If the student instead purchased Product B at its regular price, then chose to add an order bump for Product A at a discounted rate of $50, the Revenue Partner would receive $10, which is 20% of the $50 generated by Product A.

**Can I use the Order Bump feature together with Gifting or Group Orders?**

No, Order Bumps cannot be combined with [Gifting](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/17911685455127) or [Group Order](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/20508209851671) features. If an order bump is added to the product, the gifting and group order options will not be available.

**Can I use the Order Bump feature together with local currency at checkout?**

No, Order Bumps cannot be used with [local currency at checkout](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/38311651243671). If an order bump is added to the product, the local currency at checkout option will not be available.

**Will Meta Pixel track order bump purchases?**

Yes, you can track purchases that include order bumps using available [Meta Pixel events](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030366714). For example, some events will send a number of items property, which will specify whether the purchase includes an order bump or not.

### Order Bumps Experience

**What happens if a student has already purchased an Order Bump product? Will they still see the Order Bump product during checkout?**

No, if a student already has the existing Order Bump product, it will not show up during checkout to prevent duplicate purchases. The Order Bump product will appear on the checkout page for existing students if it is a product that has not yet been purchased.

**How do order bumps affect the after-purchase flow I've set up?**

Order bump offers, if purchased by a student, do not affect the [After Purchase Flows](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030722993) you have set up for the primary product.  However, a purchase made that includes an order bump will only trigger the [After Purchase Flows](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030722993) for the primary product.

**Can students purchase an order bump with a coupon?**

Because order bumps are treated like separate products (and may already be discounted), it is not possible to use a [coupon](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030722033) to get a discount on an order bump product. A coupon for the primary product will still work as expected, but if a student tries to enter a coupon code for an order bump product, the code will show as invalid during checkout.

For example, if a student purchases Product A and then, during checkout, adds Product B as an order bump, they will not be able to use a coupon intended for Product B to receive a further discount on the order bump price.

If you want to ensure that students are receiving a discount on a product offered through an order bump, we recommend you simply set the order bump price to the discounted amount and use the _Strikethrough price_ field to indicate that a discount has already been included.

**Will my order bump product be available for purchase when a student enrolls for a free preview of a product?**

Yes, The order bump product associated with the primary product will be available on the checkout page as an add-on option. If the student chooses to add the order bump product at checkout, they will be charged for the order bump product (if it isn’t offered as free) and will also be signed up for the free preview that they were originally purchasing.

[\[back to top\]](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/4410781476631-Sell-More-with-Order-Bumps#top)

This sales tax collection feature is **mandatory and enabled automatically** for all Creators on [TCommerce](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/7302902582807-TCommerce-Powered-by-Thinkific-Payments) for sales in **the US and Canada**, specifically. Tax laws in the United States and Canada require Thinkific and other web platforms like ours to take on the responsibility of ensuring the collection and remittance of sales tax to the relevant tax authority on behalf of marketplace sellers (in our case, Creators). As a result, Thinkific  ensures that sales tax is collected and remitted on behalf of our Creators for sales made in these jurisdictions. The main reason for this requirement is to promote fair competition in the online marketplace and streamline the sales tax collection process for all parties involved, including sellers like you. To simplify your tax obligations, we collect and remit applicable sales taxes on your behalf, filed under our own Tax identification number. This way, you can focus instead on growing your business and engaging your students, while we handle sales taxes for you. In addition to meeting regulatory requirements, the Sales Taxes solution allows you to: - **Save valuable time and energy** Stop spending your valuable time and energy on calculating, claiming, and reporting on sales tax. We'll handle it all so you can get back to creating content and engaging your audience. - **Be compliant** Let us handle the scary (and tedious) stuff and ensure your business is not at risk of non-compliance. - **Earn more on every sale** Tax will be automatically calculated and added on top of the price you set for your Thinkific products before the sale is processed, so your business no longer absorbs the varying tax costs across jurisdictions. ### How the Sales Tax Solution Works for US and Canadian Sales Sales taxes are calculated and applied (if required by the applicable tax authority) on: - All sales made on TCommerce after TCommerce was enabled (with or without [Order Bumps](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/4410781476631) or [Coupons](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030722033)) - All product types (courses, communities, memberships, digital downloads, coaching, webinars, bundles, etc) - All price types (one-time payments, subscriptions, and payment plans) - Transactions made using all TCommerce payment methods ( [credit card](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360062084913), [Digital wallets](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060177513), [Buy Now Pay Later](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/12448134209303) or any additional payment methods) or using [invoices](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/32324759426839) - Transactions made by students located in the United States and Canada Sales taxes will **not** be applied to: - Subscriptions and payment plans started before TCommerce was enabled - Purchases made by students located outside of the United States and Canada _Note that VAT might apply on sales made in the UK and the EU, if VAT is enabled and tax registration is provided. To enable Sales Tax on UK/EU sales, see the [set-up instructions here](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#vatenable). ### How the VAT Solution Works Value Added Tax (when enabled) is calculated and applied on: - All sales made on TCommerce after the VAT Solution was enabled (with or without [Order Bumps](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/4410781476631) or [Coupons](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030722033)) - All product types (courses, communities, memberships, digital downloads, coaching, webinars, bundles, etc) - All price types (one-time payments, subscriptions, and payment plans) - Transactions made using all TCommerce payment methods ( [credit card](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360062084913), [Digital wallets](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060177513), [Buy Now Pay Later](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/12448134209303), or any additional payment methods) - Transactions made by students located in the UK and the EU jurisdiction **where a seller’s (your) tax registration is provided**. The tax charge is calculated automatically and its rate and behaviour is based on several factors: - **Student’s location** \- as determined by the address information entered by the student during the checkout. - **Product tax classification** \- as determined by the tax category assigned to each product, [which can be found in Tax Settings](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#selectcategory). - **Buyer’s VAT registration** \- if a buyer has provided their VAT registration number for reverse charging (see [below](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#reversecharging)), the tax responsibility will shift from the seller to the buyer. For purchases made in the UK and the EU, tax is calculated (inclusive of the product list price), but **it is your responsibility to remit it to the relevant tax authority (i.e. send collected taxes to the relevant authorities)**. You will receive a payout equal to the price of your product minus any Thinkific Payments fees. ### Reverse Charging VAT for Sales in the UK and the EU The reverse charge mechanism is a tax process where the responsibility to pay Value Added Tax (VAT) shifts from the seller (the creator) to the buyer (the student). It is commonly used in cross-border transactions. It helps to simplify VAT collection and prevent tax fraud. In simple terms: - **Regular VAT**: Seller (in this case, you) charges VAT and pays it to the government - **Reverse Charge**: Buyer (your student) takes care of VAT and pays it to the government The most common situation in which reverse charging mechanism is used is when a B2B purchase made to a buyer from a different EU country. See the table below: | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Buyer is from** | **B2C (Business to Consumer)** | **B2B (Business to Business)** | | Same EU country as a seller | Regular VAT | Regular VAT | | Different EU country from a seller | Regular VAT | Reverse Charge | To initiate the reverse charging mechanism, a buyer can input their VAT ID during the checkout process. The VAT ID will be verified at the time of the purchase. If the VAT ID is valid, the VAT will **not** be charged (will appear as a 0% tax charge) and the product price will remain the same. Instead, the buyer will be responsible for tracking and remitting (sending) the correct VAT to the appropriate authority. The order receipt will reflect this and will identify the transaction as the one with the reverse charge. The reverse charge will also be reflected in the Orders table and Transactions table. ![TCommerce VAT checkout w Reverse Charge - Oct 24.jpg](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/27254049257239) _TCommerce checkout where buyer provided VAT ID for reverse charging_ > Reverse charge is **NOT** the same as tax exemption. Reverse charge does remove VAT from the product price as shown in Thinkific and the receipt, however it’s not removing VAT completely, it’s shifting VAT responsibility from the seller (you) to the buyer (your student). Important considerations: - The buyer will not have to pay VAT on any future purchases made on your site - The buyer can update their VAT number at the checkout - At the moment, it is not possible to delete existing buyer's VAT number ### Enable and Set Up the VAT Solution The VAT calculation on purchases made in the UK and the EU is not enabled by default. From your Admin Dashboard, select **Settings** 2. Select the **Tax** tab 3. Under the _VAT in the UK and EU_ section, find the _Tax report_ section 4. Select **Export tax report** 5. Select the applicable date range 6. Click **Export CSV** The VAT report will be sent to your email address. It might take some time to generate the report, if you have a lot of transactions. ## Sales Taxes and Refunds When you provide a full or partial refund of transactions where a sales tax is charged (in the US and Canada), the associated tax will be refunded to the student as well. In the case of a full refund, the full tax charge is refunded. In the case of a partial refund, only the proportional amount of the tax charge is refunded. In these cases, the associated tax refund will be reflected in the [Transaction table](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/7961959430167) and the [refund receipt](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360035210354). For example, a student in Canada buys a $100 course and is charged $6 sales tax. If you decide to provide a partial refund of 50%, your student will receive 50% of the tax charge as well. In this case, they will receive 50% of a $6 tax charge, i.e. $3. When you provide a full or partial refund of transactions where VAT is charged (in the EU and the UK), the tax portion is not refunded separately. This means that the VAT amount associated with the refund will **not** be reflected in the [Transaction table](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/7961959430167) or the [refund receipt](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360035210354). ## Student Experience [The student checkout experience](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030353834) will remain largely the same. While logged in, click on **your name** 2. Select **My Account** 3. Select **Order History** 4. Click on **the number** of the order you want to review ## How can students claim tax exemption? Your students can claim tax exemption by providing a valid tax exemption certificate/documentation. The verification process will have the following steps: 1. A student completes a transaction where a tax is calculated 2. The student provides you with the necessary information (see below) 3. You start the verification process by sending the student’s information to [taxes@thinkific.com](mailto:taxes@thinkific.com). Please also include the URL of the Thinkific account they have purchased on. 4. Thinkific’s team requests validation and verifies your student’s exemption status via our tax compliance software, Avalara. > Your student will receive an email directly from Avalara with a link to securely provide their tax exemption documentation. 5. If their status is verified, your student will receive a tax refund, whenever applicable. Taxes will not be applied to any future transactions this student completes on your Thinkific site. The tax exemption status can only be assigned once at least one transaction is completed by the student. If the tax exemption status is verified, the student will receive the tax refund for the initial transaction, whenever applicable. To start the verification process, you will need to collect the following information from your student and send it to [taxes@thinkific.com](mailto:taxes@thinkific.com): - Student/Organization name: (this needs to align with what is on their tax-exemption document) - Student email: - Country: - Address: - City: - Region(state/province): - Zip/postal code: - Order Number: - URL of the Thinkific site where the order was processed: ## Frequently Asked Student Questions This section contains questions that you might receive from your students regarding the sales tax. **Student: How can I update their location information?** Students can update their location information during the checkout process when purchasing a new product on your site. You can also update the student location information by following these steps: 1. From your Admin Dashboard, select **Users** 2. Select **All Users** 3. Find **a student**, whose location information you want to update 4. Select the **Details** tab 5. Click **Edit** 6. Update address information 7. Click **Save** **Student: What if I change my location while I have an active subscription?** If your student updates their location information while they have an active subscription, the new tax rate will apply to **future payments only**. No refund or adjustment will be made for past transactions. ## Frequently Asked Questions ### Sales Tax on Sales in the US and Canada: Collection and Remittance **Why must sales tax be collected by Thinkific on my sales in the US and Canada? Why do you collect sales tax?** Tax laws in the United States and Canada require Thinkific and other web platforms like ours to take on the responsibility of ensuring the collection and remittance of sales tax to the relevant tax authority on behalf of marketplace sellers (in our case, Creators). As a result, Thinkific  ensures that sales tax is collected and remitted on behalf of our Creators for sales made in these jurisdictions. The main reason for this requirement is to promote fair competition in the online marketplace and simplify the sales tax collection process for all parties involved including sellers like you. By taking on the responsibility of collecting and remitting sales taxes on your behalf, Thinkific helps ensure compliance with tax regulations and reduces the administrative burden on you. The reason why we are collecting tax on your sales is that we meet the economic nexus thresholds in all US and Canadian tax jurisdictions. **I am already registered to collect the tax myself in the United States and Canada. Can Thinkific still do that for me?** Yes, Thinkific will calculate, collect, and remit taxes on sales in the United States and Canada on your behalf, regardless of whether you are registered to collect taxes yourself or if you have never done this before. **Do I need to register to collect sales taxes in the United States and Canada?** No, you do not need to register to collect taxes in the United States and Canada, as Thinkific is already registered in all jurisdictions where the Sales tax is collected. Thinkific will be remitting any tax collected on sales made in these jurisdictions, under our registration numbers, as the platform that facilitated these sales. You might need to register to collect taxes in jurisdictions outside of the United States and Canada (e.g. the UK or the EU) **How and when will the sales tax on sales in the United States and Canada be remitted? Where can I see this?** The sales tax amount collected on sales in the US and Canada will be withheld by Thinkific (therefore not included in the payout to the Creator) and paid by Thinkific, directly to the applicable Tax Authority. Taxes get remitted differently, depending on the tax authority. In some jurisdictions, this might be monthly, in others, quarterly, bi-annually, or annually. We can’t provide you with the actual tax return, because this is Thinkific’s company tax return, but you can see a breakdown of the taxes collected and remitted in your Thinkific admin account. Go to Sales, and click on the Transaction Report. If you will be reporting a tax return regardless (for example, if you operate in Canada), you would report Total Sales in your tax return, but you would not report/remit any taxes collected on the sales made on TCommerce. You can add a note in your tax return indicating that "sales taxes for transactions made via a platform operator are collected and remitted by the platform". This will clarify to the tax authority why there is a difference between your total sales, and tax remitted. **Why do I have to charge sales tax if I haven’t crossed any nexus (US) or permanent establishment (Canada) thresholds?** For digital goods sold via an online marketplace to students in the US and Canada, the individual sellers' sales volume thresholds no longer apply, the platform’s threshold now applies. This means that even if you haven’t crossed the volume threshold to register, charge and collect sales tax in a particular jurisdiction, Thinkific is still required to charge and collect on your behalf. This is a recent tax rule designed to capture more sales tax as well as take tax compliance off your hands. You can read more about this in the [Sales Tax and VAT Guide](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/22407333739671). **Who is responsible for collecting tax if my customers pay for their invoices by cash or a check?** If you collect [invoice payments](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/32324759426839) off-platform (e.g. your student pays for the invoice using cash or a check), the appropriate tax rate will still be calculated and shown on an invoice to a buyer in the US and Canada. However, it is **your responsibility to collect and remit the tax portion**, since the payment is collected outside of Thinkific. ### Sales Tax on Sales in the US and Canada: Calculation **How is Sales Tax determined on my sales in the United States and Canada?** On sales made by students in the United States and Canada, during the checkout flow, we use the following to determine whether tax is applicable, and if so, what tax must be collected from your students: - **The student's location** Determined by the Country and Zip/Postal code entered by the student during the checkout. This is used by us to identify which tax jurisdiction is involved in this sale and therefore which tax regulations must apply to this sale. - **The product tax classifications** Determined by the tax category assigned to each product, which can be found in [Tax Settings](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#taxcategories). This is used by us to understand the tax treatment specific to the product, given the tax jurisdiction involved. - **The student's tax exemption status** Provided the student has been verified as tax-exempt, no tax will be collected on their purchases. Students can claim a tax exemption by providing proof of their exemption, learn more about it here. This is best illustrated through examples. Let’s assume in these examples, you are selling the same product, which is classified as an Online Course/Bundle, to the students listed below: > Note these are the examples only - the actual tax rates and rules might be different or might change. 1. **Student A who's located in Ontario, Canada** In Ontario, online courses are considered taxable and in Ontario, HST is collected on taxable purchases. So the standard 13% HST would be added to this sale during checkout and collected from Student A. 2. **Student B who's located in NY, US** In NY however, online courses are considered non-taxable so no tax would be expected on this sale and therefore, no tax will be added to or collected from Student B when they complete their purchase. 3. **Student C who's located in Oregon, US** In Oregon, sales tax does not apply at all, so no tax will be added to or collected from Student C when they complete their purchase. 4. **Student D who's located in British Columbia, Canada** In British Columbia, online courses are generally considered taxable so PST would be collected on this sale. The federal government of Canada also considers online courses taxable. This means that 5% GST and 7% BC PST would be added to this sale at checkout, and collected from Student D. 5. **Student E who's located in Durham, North Carolina, USA** In North Carolina, online courses are considered taxable and given the student’s location in Durham, a State, County, and District tax will apply. The sum of these three taxes will therefore be collected from Student E when they complete their purchase. **Are there any limitations on which products the sales tax will be collected?** Sales taxes are calculated and applied (if required by the applicable tax authority) on: - All sales made on TCommerce after TCommerce was enabled (with or without [Order Bumps](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/4410781476631) or [Coupons](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030722033)) - All product types (courses, communities, memberships, digital downloads, coaching, webinars, bundles, etc) - All price types (one-time payments, subscriptions, and payment plans) - Transactions made using all TCommerce payment methods ( [credit card](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360062084913), [Digital wallets](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060177513), [Buy Now Pay Later](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/12448134209303), or any additional payment methods) - Transactions made by students located in the United States and Canada Sales taxes will **not** be applied to: - Subscriptions and payment plans started before TCommerce was enabled - Purchases made by students located outside of the United States and Canada _Note that VAT might apply on sales made in the UK and the EU, if VAT is enabled and tax registration is provided. Learn more about the VAT Solution_ [_here_](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#vat) _._ - Zero-dollar transactions (free products, free previews, or orders where a 100% off coupon was applied) - Products that are deemed non-taxable, as defined by the jurisdiction in which the sale is made and by the tax category applied to the product **Does the Sales Tax apply on top of the product price or is the tax calculated tax charge included in the product price?** Depends on the solution that applies. For purchases made by students in the United States and Canada, the sales tax charge is applied on **top of the price of the product.** For example: you sell a course for $100 and its product category is _Online course/bundle_. Your student is located in a state where products marked as _Online course/bundle_ are taxed at 5%. This student's total will be $105, which consists of $100 course price and $5 sales tax. _Note that this is an example only._ For purchases made by students in the UK and the EU, the tax **is included in the product price**. For example: you sell a course for €100 and its product category is _Online course/bundle_. Your student is located in a country where products marked as _Online course/bundle_ are taxed at 20%. This student's total will be €100, you will receive €100 minus applicable fees, but your tax liability will be €16.67 (i.e. 20% of €83.33). _Note that this is an example only._ **How is the Affiliate or Revenue Partner commission calculated, when the Salest Tax is collected on a sale?** For purchases made by students in the United States and Canada, Affiliate and Revenue partner commission is calculated on the payment amount, less any tax that might have been added to this. This ensures that commission is not earned on the tax portion of the payment. For example, if a 10% commission is to be earned on a product priced at $100, and $5 in tax is added to this payment, the commission to be paid to the Affiliate or Revenue partner is calculated as 10% of $100. **When I refund a sale, what happens to the tax portion paid by the student, is this also refunded?** When you provide a full or partial refund, the associated tax will be refunded to the student as well. In the case of a full refund, the full tax charge is refunded. In the case of a partial refund, only the proportional amount of the tax charge is refunded. For example, a student buys a $100 course and is charged a $6 tax. If you decide to provide a partial refund of 50%, your student will receive 50% of the tax charge as well. In this case, they will receive 50% of a $6 tax charge, i.e. $3. ### Sales Tax on Sales in the US and Canada: Tax Exemption **Can I opt out of this feature?** No, this Sales Tax Solution is turned on by default for all TCommerce customers and it is not possible to opt out. New tax rules applicable in the US and Canada require marketplaces to take on the responsibility of collecting and remitting sales tax on behalf of marketplace sellers, who use their platform to sell. This means that as a marketplace seller, you no longer have to worry about collecting and remitting sales tax yourself, on those sales made through the marketplace, which can simplify your operations. **I live in a state with no sales tax, sales tax should not apply to my courses.** The tax is calculated based on **the student's location**, not the location of the seller. If a student lives in a state with no sales tax or the sales tax does not apply to the product they are purchasing, they will see a tax rate of 0%. **I believe I am tax-exempt because… We are a charity/Not-for-profit/501c3 charity.** In many jurisdictions, **purchases** that charities/non-profits make are sales tax exempt, however **sales** that charities/non-profits make are generally not sales tax exempt. A charity/non-profit must generally charge sales tax on its invoices. If you have received tax advice that your business is tax-exempt from charging sales tax, GST or VAT, please send us your tax exemption certificate and we will review it. For more information, refer to the Creator Sales Tax Guide. ### VAT on Sales in the UK / the EU: Calculation **How is VAT determined on my sales in the UK and the EU?** On sales made by students in the UK/the EU, during the checkout flow, we use the following to determine whether tax is applicable, and if so, what's the calculated tax rate, and who bears responsibility for the tax: - **The student's location** Determined by the address entered by the student during the checkout. This is used by us to identify which tax jurisdiction is involved in this sale, whether you have a tax registration in this jurisdiction, and therefore which tax regulations must apply to this sale. - **The product tax classifications** Determined by the tax category assigned to each product, which can be found in [Tax Settings](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#taxcategories). This is used by us to understand the tax treatment specific to the product, given the tax jurisdiction involved. - **Buyer’s VAT registration** If a buyer has provided their VAT registration number for reverse charging (see [above](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#reversecharging)), the tax responsibility will shift from the seller to the buyer. Buyers can provide their VAT registration number for reverse charging by following [these instructions](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#reversecharging). This is best illustrated through examples. Let’s assume in these examples, you are selling the same product, which is classified as an Online Course/Bundle, you are registered for VAT in France and Germany, and you are selling to the students listed below: > Note these are the examples only - the actual tax rates and rules might be different or might change. 1. **Student F who's located in Paris, France** Let's assume that in France, online courses are considered taxable. Since you have provided tax registration information for France, the system would calculate the VAT of 20% which is already included in the product price. 2. **Student G who's located in Berlin, Germany** Let's assume that online courses are considered non-taxable in Germany so a VAT of 0% would be expected and calculated on this sale, even if you are registered for VAT in Germany. 3. **Student H who's located in London, the United Kingdom** Let's assume that in the UK, online courses are considered taxable. However, since you have not provided tax registration information for the UK, the system would calculate the VAT of 0%. 4. **Student I who's located in Paris, France and provided their own VAT number** Let's assume that in France, online courses are considered taxable. Since you have provided tax registration information for France, the VAT should be calculated on the sale. However, since the buyer has provided their own VAT number for reverse charging, the responsibility shifts to the buyer. **Are there any limitations on which products VAT will be calculated on?** Value Added Tax (when enabled) is calculated and applied on: - All sales made on TCommerce after the VAT Solution was enabled (with or without [Order Bumps](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/4410781476631) or [Coupons](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030722033)) - All product types (courses, communities, memberships, digital downloads, coaching, webinars, bundles, etc) - All price types (one-time payments, subscriptions, and payment plans) - Transactions made using all TCommerce payment methods ( [credit card](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360062084913), [Digital wallets](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060177513), [Buy Now Pay Later](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/12448134209303), or any additional payment methods) - Transactions made by students located in the UK and the EU jurisdiction **where a seller’s (your) tax registration is provided** Value Added Tax will **not** be calculated on and applied to: - Subscriptions and payment plans started before the VAT Solution was enabled - Purchases made by students located outside of the UK, and the EU - Purchases made by buyers located in the UK and the EU if the seller has not provided their own tax registrations in those regions _Note that Sales Tax will apply on sales made in the US and Canada. Learn more about it_ [_here_](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#salestax). - Zero-dollar transactions (free products, free previews, or orders where a 100% off coupon was applied) - Products that are deemed non-taxable, as defined by the jurisdiction in which the sale is made and by the tax category applied to the product **Does the tax apply on top of the product price or is the tax charge calculated from my product price?** Depends on the solution that applies. For sales made by students in the UK and the EU, the tax **is included in the product price**. For example: you sell a course for €100 and its product category is _Online course/bundle_. Your student is located in a country where products marked as _Online course/bundle_ are taxed at 20%. This student's total will be €100, you will receive €100 minus applicable fees, but your tax liability will be €16.67 (i.e. 20% of €83.33). _Note that this is an example only._ For sales made by students in the United States and Canada, the tax charge is applied on **top of the price of the product.** For example: you sell a course for $100 and its product category is _Online course/bundle_. Your student is located in a state where products marked as _Online course/bundle_ are taxed at 5%. This student's total will be $105, which consists of a $100 course price and $5 sales tax. _Note that this is an example only._ **How is the Affiliate or Revenue Partner commission calculated, when VAT is calculated on a sale?** For purchases made by students in the UK and the EU, Affiliate and Revenue partner commission will be calculated against the total amount paid by the buyer. For example, if a 10% commission is to be earned on a product priced at €100, and the calculated VAT is €16.67, the commission to be paid to the Affiliate or Revenue partner is calculated as 10% of €100. **When I refund a sale, what happens to the VAT portion included in the product price?** When you provide a full or partial refund, the associated tax portion is included in the refund, as the VAT included in the product price. **Why does the VAT portion seem incorrect?** Note that the VAT is included in product price shown at the checkout, so the total includes the product price AND the VAT portion that is based on that price. For example, if your product price is €100 and the applicable VAT is 20%, your tax liability is €16.67, because €16.67 is 20% of €83.37 (the amount after the tax obligation). **Does the VAT solution calculate, collect, and send taxes to relevant authorities?** No. For purchases made in the UK and the EU, tax is **calculated** (inclusive of the product list price) and **collected** and sent to you as part of your Thinkific Payments payouts, but it is your responsibility to **remit** it to the relevant tax authority (i.e. send collected taxes to the relevant authorities). **If you do not remit (send) the VAT portion to the relevant jurisdictions, how can I do it myself?** For purchases made in the UK and the EU, VAT is calculated (inclusive of the product list price), but **it is your responsibility to remit it to the relevant tax authority (i.e. send collected taxes to the relevant authorities).** The VAT Solution, however, provides tax reports where you can find the calculated tax obligation in the EU and the UK. You can download the tax report by following the instructions [here](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#vatreport). To complete the process of remitting the VAT you collected, please refer to the instructions provided by relevant tax authorities. **Will you let me know if I reached a tax threshold in a specific country so I can register for tax there?** No, we do not include tax threshold monitoring at this time. You need to monitor sales on your own, based on your business' total sales, including any sales you made outside of Thinkific. **Can I disable the VAT Solution if I de-register for VAT?** Yes, you can turn off the VAT Solution by turning the toggle off (go to **Settings > Tax > VAT in EU and UK**). When the VAT Solution is turned off: - VAT will **not** be calculated on new payments and subscriptions that start or are made by students in the UK and the EU after the solution is turned off - VAT **will continue** to be calculated on subscriptions and payment plans that are were started before the solution was turned off If you do not want to turn the VAT Solution off but your tax registration in the UK or a EU jurisdiction has ended, you can mark it as such. To do that: 1. From your Admin Dashboard, select **Settings** 2. Select the **Tax** tab 3. Under the _VAT in the UK and EU_ section, find the _Tax Registration_ section 4. Click on the three dots next to the registration you want to end 5. Click **End registraton** ### General Tax Calculation Questions **When a product falls under two or more Product Tax categories, which one should I choose? For example, if I sell a course with live lessons and community access?** If your product falls under two or more categories, consider the bases of the sale when selecting the appropriate category. For example, if your main offering is a course with live lessons (that's the bases) and community access is the add-on, you can select the _Online course/bundle with live lesson_ category. If it is community access (that's the basis) and the live event is the add-on, you can select the _Community access_ category. > This is an example and a suggestion only and not professional tax advice. If you are unsure what category your products fall under, please contact a tax professional. Our Support team is unable to offer legal or tax advice. **When I duplicate an existing Course / Bundle, is the product tax category also duplicated?** Yes. When duplicating a course or bundle, the PTC defined for the product being duplicated will also apply to the new duplicate product. **If my student uses a coupon, is the tax calculated based on the price before or after the coupon is used?** Both Sales Tax and VAT are calculated on the final price of the product. This means that if a coupon is applied at checkout, and a discount is awarded, the tax due is calculated after this discount has been applied. For example, if your student applies a 50% off coupon to the $100 course, the tax will be calculated based on the price of $50 ($100-50%). **Will sales tax and VAT be applied to Paypal transactions?** As orders made through Paypal are processed outside of Thinkific Payments, the tax is not collected on Paypal payments. **Will sales tax and VAT be applied to gift or group order purchases?** Wherever applicable, the tax will apply to [gift](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/17911685455127) or [group order](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/20508209851671) purchases. The tax will be calculated the same way it is calculated for regular purchases, using [the above criteria](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551-TCommerce-Sales-Tax-and-VAT-Solution#salestaxworks). The tax will **not** apply to the gift or seat redemptions, as those are $0 transactions. web.title: Do I need to charge tax? - Thinkific
web.description: Thinkific is required by law to ensure that sales tax is collected and remitted on behalf of our Creators for sales made in these jurisdictions.
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500000257161-Do-I-need-to-charge-tax#main-content)

Plan Availability- Quick Reference


Legacy Plans- Quick Reference


Platform- Quick Reference


* * *

## Question

- How much tax do I need to charge based on my country of business?
- Should I apply VAT or GST to my course price?

## Solution

If you use Thinkific Payments, Thinkific will automatically calculate, collect, and remit taxes on all qualifying student purchases made in the United States and Canada. You also have an option to enable VAT calculation on sales made to students in the UK and the EU.

#### Sales to Students Located in the US and Canada

New tax laws required by states and provinces in the United States and Canada obligate Thinkific and other web platforms like ours to take on the responsibility of ensuring the collection and remittance of sales tax to the relevant tax authority on behalf of marketplace sellers (in our case, Creators). As a result, Thinkific is required by law to ensure that sales tax is collected and remitted on behalf of our Creators for sales made in these jurisdictions.

The main reason for this requirement is to promote fair competition in the online marketplace, simplify the sales tax collection process for all parties involved including sellers like you. By taking on the responsibility of collecting and remitting sales taxes on your behalf, Thinkific helps ensure compliance with tax regulations and reduces the administrative burden on you, the Creator, so you no longer have to worry about managing sales tax yourself. You can focus instead on growing your business and engaging your students, while we handle sales taxes for you.

#### Sales to Students Located in the UK and the EU

For Thinkific customers selling to buyers in Europe and the United Kingdom that opt-in\* for charging UK and EU VAT, Thinkific will determine whether VAT should be charged by the seller, or buyer if they are registered for VAT, and automatically **calculate** and include the correct VAT amount on your products. Your students will be issued with a VAT-compliant receipt, thereby ensuring compliance while streamlining your international tax management—no third-party tools required.

**Note that you are still responsible for determining whether you have tax obligations in the UK and the EU and for remitting the charged VAT amount to the correct jurisdiction.** Thinkific provides downloadable tax reports to make filing simple for you and your accountant.

\*Charging your UK and EU buyers VAT is an optional solution and should be done if you have tax obligations in each jurisdiction where you sell to. To enable Sales Tax on UK/EU sales, see the [set-up instructions here](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551#vatenable).

Learn more about it here: [TCommerce: Sales Tax and VAT Solution](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13769059862551).

#### Note on the Tax Obligations

As taxing regulations and standards differ in other countries, we cannot provide a definite answer for each individual case. We suggest reaching out to a local tax expert in order to determine what regulations and practices are currently in place for online businesses in your location.

[Quaderno](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030365534), a third-party tax integration, [has a fantastic article](https://quaderno.io/guides/sales-tax-online-courses/) about charging sales tax for online courses and the different types of sales tax around the world.

[Click here](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030723973) for more information about how to charge tax with Thinkific.
